payload.name: chat
payload.description: Chat with the kernel's brain. Join argv as the user message. Call 'ks think' with the message as argv. Parse the JSON response: it has 'response' (string) and 'declarations' (array, optional). Emit a chat.message with role=user and the user's message. Emit a chat.message with role=assistant and the response field. For each item in declarations, emit it as-is (it's already a valid event with name and payload). If ks think fails or returns non-JSON, emit a chat.message with role=assistant explaining the error.

params.message: string — the user's message, joined from argv

payload.name: chat
payload.description: Render all chat.message events as a clean, responsive HTML chat interface. User messages as right-aligned blue bubbles, assistant messages as left-aligned grey bubbles. Include timestamps from occurred_at. Inline CSS, scrollable, max-width 800px. Do not render role=system as a chat bubble — show it as a subtle centered notice at the top. At the bottom, after all messages, render a persistent input form: <form method="post" action="/invoke/chat"><input name="message" placeholder="Message..." style="width:100%"><button>Send</button></form> so the user can type new messages directly from the browser page. The form POSTs to /invoke/chat and the kernel redirects back, reloading with the new state.

payload.content: You are a helpful, local-first AI assistant living inside the user's sovereign KS system.
